title: Tinned Tuna Tostada
summary: This simple, but effective, tuna tostada recipe is as affordable as it is delicious. A speedy high-protein pescatarian weeknight dinner.

method.description: To crisp up the tortillas, you can use two different methods: fry or bake them. To bake them, preheat the oven to 200°C, brush each tortilla with oil on both sides, place onto a baking tray and roast in the oven for 10-15 mins, flipping halfway through until golden and crispy. If you’re frying, place a frying pan over a medium-high heat with 1cm oil. Once hot, fry the tortillas for 2-3 mins until golden and crispy. Transfer to a baking tray lined with kitchen paper.

Stir the mayo and hot sauce in a large bowl. Drain the tuna and add that, too. Trim and thinly slice the spring onion and cucumber, add to the bowl and mix well. Season with salt. Thinly slice the avocado.

For the vinaigrette, halve and finely grate the tomatoes into another bowl, discarding the skin. Squeeze in the juice of ½ a lime, then add the remaining vinaigrette ingredients and stir well. Taste and season with salt, adding more lime juice if needed.
Pile the tuna mixture on top of the tostadas. Place a bit of avocado on, and spoon over the tomato dressing. Serve with any remaining lime wedges.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
